Copy Blue Print - Week 4 – Session 8
Most important thing from copywriter teleseminar –
Starting the VSL or email or letter in the middle of a story. This creates an open loop and allows
for the reader / viewer to be pulled in. Curiosity creates attention.
6:20
Hot seats:
First up – Derek
Topic – this needs to be added to the niche section
Succeed as an author
Thriving business (May not really be a primary goal for a writer – more like a secondary goal
8:00
When dealing with similar avatars go to the niche section and copy the information inside. Now
create a similar avatar with most of the same content.
Jon knows the primary goal of a buyer for “fit over 40” is to not to see their abs – most people
over 40 do not care about seeing their abs. This may be a secondary goal for some of the readers
in the avatar.
Dream come true (not a primary goal) when people wake up in the morning they do not think in
these words. What is it they say when they wake up in the morning and think about their
problem. Life of your dreams is a better way to say this (leave the pronoun blank)
Pick the most powerful primary goal and stick with it inside the software.
Primary goals are usually easy it is the secondary goals that are tricky to nail down.
Primary goal + everything else they want with it
10:30
What is the topic 2-3 words max (one is the best when possible)
Hey, speaking of _________ I have some good information to share…
This is how you come up with the topic. The topics take some time to get used to.
Derek - topic entry (book sales)
Jon – topic entry (writing) big picture and get clever (watch the videos since Jon explains how to
enter the information. Look for words that are used all of the time and already in the lexicon of
the avatar.
LeAnn – topic entry (author authority) this may work or way not
13:45
Secondary goals
Share your writing with the world (this works)
If I become a best-selling author and share my writing with the world
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 7 --

“reach more readers” is OK – “Impact lives” is a better way of saying it.
· 	Publish with confidence
· 	Make money
· 	Make positive impact
Jon – (notes on filling in the sections) When filling in the fields in each section do as many as
possible at one time since the thought process is already going. If I come back I may not
remember the thought I was on and it may make it difficult to remember what I was writing
about.
More readers vs life changing (make sure to not use verbs)
Becoming a speaker vs sharing your ideas from stage (primary goal) not all writers want to be
speakers – know your avatar
17:30 - Brennan
Dominate email sales – Jon liked the word “dominate”
Email marketing could also be a niche
Email domination may be a better way to explain the topic instead of email marketing
Copypro notes
Primary goals section
Look for ways to connect the ideas in the topic section the primary goal of “your email
marketing goals” connects with the topic of “goal setting”
Marketing competition (primary goal) flows through to marketing mastery (topic)
22:00
Secondary goals
· 	Increase customer engagement
· 	Increase fanbase
· 	Increase list size
Increase is a verb that works but a juicy verb works better
Double is much better
Double your list size is much more impactful than
23:45
Copy lesson from Jon – Great copy does not ask to you imagine anything too hard. Increasing
fan base is hard to think of as a visualization. Double your list size does not require much to
think about. Write copy in a way that makes it easy for the reader to visualize. Help them be lazy
but guide their thoughts. Look for ways to make the word specific and juicy. Specificity in copy
is the goal (it rules)
Dominate email marketing - is close enough
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

Double you income - is better
25:00
Joshua Pelicier – Joined the call
Neil Strauss letter (The Game – PUA) (swiped.com/file/annihilation-launch-letter)
“You can’t buy this secret system unless you swear to never reveal it to anyone else” this makes
you be part of the club. (Scott Haines wrote this copy)
Only 375 insiders will ever be exposed to the privately held game techniques.. (more unique
information – this is an ascension model, the participant needs to be worthy to jump through the
hoops)
Use primary goals that are different than what the rest of the market uses to gain attention.
26:50 – Jason Fladilen copy (swiped.co/file/third-person-sales-letter-from-jason-fladilen/)
“For people who want more…
… The small and easy productivity tweaks you can use as soon as tomorrow morning to get a lot
more done… with way less effort” – this is an obvious style of headline
Writing as a 3rd person
Among other things I was able to work with Jason
Jons copywriting tip
When stuck do an interview a friend or client and write in their voice through their answer.
· 	Long form emails in 3-4 hours, fully formatted
· 	Email swipes in 15 minutes
· 	Complete marketing strategies in 40 minutes
Imagine the following scenario “You’re able to let go of your overwhelm. You’re able to breeze
through your toto list. Achieve all of your goals almost effortlessly – every day.
Secondary and Primary goals are evident in the copy
29:00
Jons copywriting tip
Imagine goes into the right brain and then … able, achieve (commands and future pacing)
A better way to write the copy could include:
· 	You achieve all of your goals
· 	You are completely productive
· 	Your mind is always present in the now
These examples are all future pacing – the mind hears this as it is happening right now. The
power of future pacing goals is a samurai tactic. This what your life is like today.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

“Imagine the following scenario … someday soon you will be able to let go of your overwhelm.
After you read this course you will be able to breeze though your to do list. (this is way harder
than what is above in the bullets.
31:10 – John Carlton – Goal centric copy
Jons copywriting tip – speak in terms of one thing and not multiple things. What is one thing
that will improve copy is better than what are a bunch of things than can improve copy.
John Carlton uses this with “how one simple” in the opening of his headline..
How one simple “magic move” (which you can easily feed into your current swing
in just 9 minutes, even if you stink at the game right now) Instantly, so much
hidden raw power, balance and accuracy… That you can go out tomorrow and
launch an pin point 230 yard tee shot with a 3 wood…From your knees”
Stick to the power of one thing (this captures attention)
The primary goal is to play better golf or have a better swing – he doesn’t have to say it. He used
words around the primary goal.
Goal after goal is used in the copy John Carlton wrote.
33:00
Good copy is hyper specific
· 	Increase your sales - is good
· 	Double your sales - is better
· 	Go home and look at an extra zero to your bank account - is best
· 	One simple technique to add a zero to your bank account in 7 days - is killer
34:00
I want to help you feel better vs I want to help you feel so good you can dance again
Massive distinctions are made in copy pro
Choppy copy + specific copy = great copy (this is what John Carlton does)
Here is a line from Carltons copy… “Best part: he works fast. No nonsense”
Have 1 – 2 super specific lines in the copy “Launch a pinpoint 230 yard tee shot with a 3 wood”
is super specific
Do not be super choppy in headline copy – create something that is impossible to not see.
36:00
Headlines (swiped.com/file/from-your-knees-golf-ad-by-john-calrton/)
Be super to the point
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

Know the ulterior motive or bragging rights of the avatar (what gives them status – what would
they brag about to friends at the bar – what would they want friends to brag about them
__________ (name) did this and you would not believe it).
Paint a picture that is impossible to misunderstand – use this one time to grab shock or attention
Headline formula
You can go out tomorrow and ___________ (primary goal)
· 	You can go out tomorrow and cinch a rear naked choke against a proficient black belt and
get him to tap out immediately
· 	You can go out tomorrow and train your dog
John used goals throughout all of his copy
Look for primary and secondary complaints and where they can be used in the copy – Where can
they be used in the headline or the copy to get the most benefit.
“Whacking the stuffing out of the ball” – this is a great way to say what they want.
How can you say the primary goal in short form and long form
Short – Whack the stuffing out of the ball
Long - Launch a pinpoint 230 yard tee shot with a 3 wood
41:15
Learn how to be choppy and concise in emails and now expand them (complaints, primary and
secondary goals) at least though the primary goal do one for homework – Don’t be John Carlton
but come close.
Visceral and juicy verbs
Hit a really long drive
Smash a super long drive
Google – Find juicy verbs, find visceral verbs
What is the difference between
· 	Blister
· 	Hit
· 	Smash
· 	Whack
Dominate is a good verb since it is visceral
Verb examples
Improve my swing vs. hit the ball further vs. launch the ball further
Take the copy and then look for the next level to make it more visceral
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

Homework – Find a primary and secondary goal worded in a way that grabs the reader and
draws them in
Juicy verbs lead people to want to see what something is even if they don’t like it. Jon doesn’t
like golf but he can see what John Carlton is saying. Write in a way so people who may not like
the topic will want to read it. If a non avatar gets pulled into the copy and wants to read the target
avatar would like to read the copy as well.
48:15
Hot tip for copywriter teleseminar
Instead of Dear friend, start in the middle of the story. This grabs attention and isn’t vanilla plain.
Mr. Money fingers Chris Haddad brought this topic up. (Chris, Paris and Jon do this)
“It was right then I knew she cheated on me and it was all my fault” – I knew what happened
but I don’t know how
“She burst into my office screaming… I reach for the phone to dial 911… her face was turning
red… I thought she was dying but it was worse than what I imagined. Ill tell you what happened
to my assistant Sharon in a few minutes, right after I share the dangers of …” – This is for Jons 4
day diet product (people want to know what happened before the incident)
Hollywood does this all of the time
This is a pattern interrupt (She burst in the door) + Massive open loop (I will tell you in a few
minutes)
When I looked at her and what she held in her hand, I said to myself “I may repent of this on my
death bed”… and I still may … but it took my mother’s pain away” – CBD oil product (what
would I do to make this happen.. did I sell my soul) The reader will want to know what is being
written about.
55:30
Questions and answers
1. 	Comment about John Carlton – Tim G wanted to know who wrote the ad as soon as he
read it when he worked for Gold digest.
2. 	Derick- does this work for a primary goal “title”
Hint – watch your phone light up with first name celebrities as your book skyrockets to the top
(make the words visceral) break the rules of trimming it down for this exercise.
There are times where rules have to be bent or broken – Know when this needs to be done.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 7 --

3. 	Magic pill headline formula – Can a magic pill technique be used to help build a better
headline “If you could wave a magic mind / snap your fingers to __________” now write it out
as if it were true.
· 	Go from crazy town to real when using this formula.
· 	Crazy pill to secret when doing this. Go from ridiculous to what can really be done.
· 	Promises and goals are the same thing just worded differently
· 	The primary goal to be written is on the customer would have thought themselves but if
they hear it say “Give it to me right now”
“I wish I had power back in my game” –
Communicate in a way that gets their inner drive excited
4. 	When to use stories vs empathy – this is complicated and this is part of the curriculum.
An engaging story teller can write good stories. When in doubt start in the middle of the story.
Sir Gary Halbert used mini stories – know the avatar and what will get their attention and get
them excited.
The secret of the oldest person who ever lived - he tied down one sentence after another
(chiasmas)
Homework – rebuild a primary goal with juicy content
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
